quiz.question: SQL logical execution order starts with…
quiz.explanation: FROM/JOIN → WHERE → GROUP BY → HAVING → SELECT → ORDER BY → LIMIT.

quiz.question: The N+1 problem means…
options: 1 query for parents + N queries for children/associations
quiz.explanation: Classic ORM pitfall; fix with JOIN FETCH, EntityGraph, or DTO projections.

quiz.question: Composite index (user_id, status) helps queries that filter on…
options: user_id alone, or user_id + status (leftmost prefix)
quiz.explanation: B-tree composite indexes follow the leftmost-prefix rule.

quiz.question: @Transactional on a private method in Spring…
options: Is a silent no-op (AOP proxies public methods)
Rolls back checked exceptions by default
quiz.explanation: Spring AOP only intercepts calls through the proxy on public methods.

quiz.question: ACID Atomicity in a bank transfer means…
options: Both debit and credit commit, or neither does
Reads always see latest writes globally
quiz.explanation: All-or-nothing: failure rolls back the whole transaction.

problems.title: Fix N+1 in JPA
problems.prompt: findAll() users then calling user.getOrders() causes 1001 queries. Write the JPQL approach to fix it.
problems.solution: @Query("SELECT DISTINCT u FROM User u JOIN FETCH u.orders") — loads users + orders in one SQL round trip.

problems.title: Index Decision
problems.prompt: EXPLAIN shows Seq Scan on a 500k-row orders table filtered by user_id and status. What index do you add?
hints: Order columns by selectivity / query pattern.
problems.solution: CREATE INDEX idx_orders_user_status ON orders(user_id, status);
